title: Gift Wrapping Station Wall Shelf Organizer
summary: A wall-mounted shelf with drilled holes and dowels that hold wrapping paper rolls upright and organized, plus shelves for tape, scissors, and ribbon.
description:
The dowel holes are the one detail that needs real precision — bored with a spade or Forstner bit sized exactly to a 3/4" dowel, since a loose hole lets the dowel (and every roll of wrapping paper hanging on it) tip and slide. Mark the hole centers carefully before drilling, and cut the corners off the top and bottom afterward for a softer profile.

Everything else is straightforward shelf-and-divider construction, screwed to studs through the back panel since a fully loaded wrapping station — paper rolls, ribbon, tape, scissors — carries real weight for a wall-mounted piece.

tools.note: A 3/4" spade or Forstner bit is what actually matters here — size it exactly to your dowels.
Softens the top and bottom corners after drilling.

cutList.note: Mark and drill the 3/4" dowel holes before cutting the top and bottom corners.

steps.title: Drill the dowel holes
steps.body: Measure and mark the hole centers on both side panels. Drill with a 3/4" spade or Forstner bit sized exactly to the dowels — a loose hole lets the dowel (and everything hanging on it) tip. Cut the top and bottom corners off each side with a jigsaw or circular saw for a softer profile.

steps.title: Build the case
steps.body: Attach the sides to the top and bottom.

steps.title: Add the shelves
steps.body: Attach the main shelves to the sides.

steps.title: Add the divider
steps.body: Attach the divider to the shelves.

steps.title: Hang and add dowels
steps.body: Hang the organizer on the wall through the back panel into studs, using 3" screws. Insert the dowels into the drilled holes to hold wrapping paper rolls upright.

images.alt: Wall-mounted gift wrapping station with dowel rod holders
